plainLanguageDirection: Make the astronaut actually run toward the camera/in place: one foot supports while the other knee drives forward, arms pump opposite the legs, the body bounces from real contact. Keep the camera fixed and the character full-body.
rhythm: two clear contact downbeats plus lifted-knee peaks in a 24-frame loop; energetic cartoon run, not a slow march

physics.centerOfMass: small vertical bounce tied to support contact, no random drifting
physics.contacts: at least one readable support foot on contact frames; brief float allowed only between contacts
physics.arms: opposite arm/leg coordination; elbows bend instead of rubber stretching
physics.headHelmet: head and helmet remain coherent, with only slight follow-through

negativeDirection: do not twist the whole body like a liquify warp
do not keep both legs straight while only moving pixels
do not crop helmet, boots, or hands
do not add extra limbs, duplicate boots, broken knees, or detached gloves
do not turn the character into side view or three-quarter view
do not change to a different costume or character
